name: Yam

disambiguationNotes: Canonical FoodRanked yam means true yam, not sweet potato.
Do not substitute sweet potato data.
Use this entry for the project's chosen public-facing yam baseline and keep any future species-specific refinements as explicit separate entries if needed.

secondarySources.note: Exact SR Legacy row identified in this pass. This improves auditability, but it does not fully solve the broader market/species ambiguity around 'yam'.

changeNotes: Production draft upgraded with explicit USDA anchor fdcId 170071 / ndbNumber 11601.
Canonical FoodRanked yam is now locked to true yam rather than sweet potato.

This file now treats the attached USDA row as the current primary source anchor for that chosen public-facing yam identity, with any future species refinement handled as an explicit follow-up rather than an unresolved blocker.

kcal.sourceName: USDA FoodData Central API food details
kcal.note: Canonical anchor row for energy. Current file value 118 matches the attached row. Derivation is calculated (NC/source 4).

protein_g.sourceName: USDA FoodData Central API food details
protein_g.note: Canonical anchor row for protein. Current file value 1.5 is aligned with attached row value 1.53.

potassium_dv.sourceName: USDA FoodData Central API food details
potassium_dv.note: Canonical anchor row for potassium. DV conversion updated conservatively to 17% based on current project DV assumptions.

omega3_mg.sourceName: USDA FoodData Central API food details
omega3_mg.note: Adjusted to a conservative ~12 mg total omega-3-equivalent from the attached yam fatty-acid rows.

cholesterol_mg.sourceName: USDA FoodData Central API food details
cholesterol_mg.note: Plant-food cholesterol is explicitly 0 mg on the attached USDA row, so this should be 0 rather than null.

essential_amino_acids_score.note: Displayed as N/A for now. No defensible reusable amino-acid scoring method has been attached for this exact food identity.

nonessential_amino_acids_score.note: Displayed as N/A for now. No defensible reusable amino-acid scoring method has been attached for this exact food identity.

glycemic_index.note: Displayed as N/A for now. GI should not be trusted until the exact yam species and preparation basis are both locked.

bioavailability_percent.note: Displayed as N/A for now. This metric remains part of the final display schema, but the project does not yet have a defensible sourced method for yam bioavailability_percent.

pros.title: can offer useful fibre for a starch-heavy root
pros.explanation: That is directionally fair from the current locked yam draft profile.

evidence.citationNote: Supported by the current locked yam draft profile, while still remaining open to later source refinement if a more exact species-specific row is adopted.

pros.title: appears to bring a decent potassium return
pros.explanation: A fair upside from the current locked yam profile, but still not something to oversell into elite status.

evidence.citationNote: Supported by the current locked yam draft profile, while still remaining open to later source refinement if a more exact species-specific row is adopted.

pros.title: whole-food staple value is plausible
pros.explanation: This is reasonable editorial framing but should not be treated as proof that the current draft profile is secure.

evidence.citationNote: Keep non-authoritative until identity is resolved.

cons.title: public naming confusion makes careful labeling important
cons.explanation: Yam is commonly confused with sweet potato in public use, so the project has to keep the identity labeling explicit even after choosing a canonical yam entry.

evidence.sourceName: project identity policy plus public naming reality
evidence.citationNote: This remains a real caution, but no longer blocks the entry because the project has now chosen a canonical true-yam identity.

cons.title: micronutrient return should not be assumed elite
cons.explanation: Reasonable caution against overselling even after locking the canonical true-yam identity.

evidence.citationNote: Fair caution based on the current locked nutrient panel.

cons.title: it is still mainly an energy food
cons.explanation: Fair category framing based on the current locked yam profile.

evidence.citationNote: Directionally fair based on the current locked nutrient panel.

primaryRemainingBlockers: Attach a defensible GI source for the chosen true-yam identity or keep GI as explicit N/A.
Keep public-facing labeling explicit so this entry is not confused with sweet potato.
Treat future species-specific yam entries as separate additions rather than rewrites of this canonical baseline.

sourceNotes: Draft production-lane true-yam entry with USDA anchor fdcId 170071 / ndbNumber 11601.
Canonical FoodRanked yam is now locked to true yam rather than sweet potato.
This row is being used as the current primary source anchor for that public-facing yam baseline.
